div.p: " hÃŧËģi 'ÃŪËģËģ hÃŧËģi 'ÃŪËģËģ " nikÉÂīni he says n.
* The character described in this story has a name but the informant has forgotten what it is.
He thought of this character in connection with " Le juif errant."
koÂīmeli = water elm? charmille. de carne.i
koÂīyaca = bois de smel serpent - poss. cenelle

tr.td: when they opened and got out
when they opened and got out
I can't stay with you all?
I can't stay with you all?
I can't stay with you all?
I can't stay with you all?
back of her he went (he followed her)
back of her he went (he followed her)

div.p: winaÂīcinik'ahtca = I'm going to lead (take) you
'uÂīkiwÄ yaÂīk'ahkÄŊ = I'm going to stay; I intend to stay
yaÂīpa'er'uwa = he's carrying it on his shoulder
yaÂīpanik'ahtca = I'm going to carry it
yaÂīpawik'ahtca = he's going to carry it
yaÂīmap'ÉĖĻta = they're already dressed (they done dressed)
taRkuk yÉÂītahk'ura = the tree's getting rotten
taÂīhaLkitc 'uwatc'Éhkini = I pulled it out of the ground
taÂīhaLkitc 'uwaÂītcunik'aÂīhtca = I'm going to pull it out of the ground
taÂīwickitc 'uÂīwatc'Éhkini = I pulled it out of water
haÂīËģic 'uÂīwatc'Éhkini = I pulled it out of the air
kaÂīnahk'Écka waÂītc'Éhkini = I pulled out some kind of root

tr.td: they helped him (all the time) was in the habit of
they helped him (all the time) was in the habit of

div.p: haÂīrawi paÂīkahk'Ä ta = The Alligator Dance the leader having sung, they "answer"
The Avoyelle Woman Who Never Died

tr.td: getting up, he came to see
getting up, he came to see

div.p: taÂīl'ukin hoÂītuc = at the end of the village
nÃĪÂīca = mud-fish ( better, but the other sounds good, too)
taÂīhtc'i miÂīr'ata = the sun cleared up
taÂīkohina tcuÂī'ÉÂīhkini = I take a cup
taÂīik hoÂīn'uhki = the fire is out
taÂīik 'ÃĪhaku = the fire is lighting (like a lantern)
taÂīik waÂīnaku = the fire is burning
taÂīRkuk waÂīnahoÂīt'uÂīhki = the wood is burned up
kaÂīta wiÂīyÄ = Where are you going?
kaÂī'ac yaÂīk'ik'aÂīhtcÄ = What day you coming back?
tiÂīhtcÄŊ kaÂīnahk 'aÂītÄ = How is she getting along?
'uÂīwÄŊ kaÂīnahk 'uÂīwÄ = How is he getting along?
teÂīwali yaÂīkawik'aÂīhtcÄ = Is he coming back soon?
kaÂīfi yaÂīca kÉÂīrakati = she drinks her coffee bitter (unsweetened)
taÂīitaÂīwÃĪhak 'uÂīwaÂī = ââthe lightning bug is going
Sam says he forgets the names of the days of the week and the names of the months but that the old folks used to know them
taÂīhtc'a wÃĪÂīkahk'aÂīra = the moon is changing, becoming new
laÂīspi 'ik'aÂīhtca le'uta = I haven't any money
laÂīspik 'uk'aÂīhtca le'uta = he doesn't have any money
tik'aÂīhtca le'uta = she hasn't any money
sÃĪÂīmÄ laÂīspi 'aÂīrale'uta = they haven't any money
A trutle has seven kinds of meat
taÂīyoronik The Tunica n 'uhtiÂīrarÉÂīwa on his flag 'uhaÂīlan'uÂī'uhkÃĪÂīni he drew the picture (of the eagle). v.II.
kaÂīpac naÂīmu 'iÂīlaka = I have right smart chickens
kaÂīpac 'ilakaleÂīuta = I ain't got no more chickens
kaÂīpac naÂīmu 'ik'ara = I got many chickens

tr.td: he drew the picture (of the eagle).

div.p: kaÂīpac naÂīmu 'ik'aÂīra = I have many chickens
kaÂīpac naÂīmu 'ilaÂīka = I have many chickens
riÂīhkukoÂīhku = 2 words for the same thing
tiÂīhtciru poÂīkani = I see a wren
tiÂīhtciru 'iÂīli poÂīkani = I see two wrens
taÂītihtciru haÂīrahk'aÂīra = the wren is singing

tr.td: I'm going to fix you good
I'm going to fix you good

div.p: tiÂīhtcÄŊ laÂīpu tipoÂīkani = I like her
kaÂīnahk taÂīp'ahkÄŊ maÂīrikani = An answer to "harvest"
'aÂīcu paÂīn'Éki 'ic'ÃĪÂīpatik'aÂīhtca = Next week I'm going to like him
tiÂīhtcÄŊ 'ic'ÃĪÂīpa poÂī laÂīpu paÂīnu = I love her very much
taÂīwici naÂīmu paÂīnu = a whole lot of water
taÂīwicÃĪna weÂīn'ÃĪÂīhkini taÂīRku kiÂītcÅģ = I found a lake in the woods
kaÂīnahk yaÂīnakani = I'm speaking for something
taÂīsak 'uÂīra tiÂīcuhk tiÂīric = The dog lies in front of the door
toÂīnik peÂīl'uÂīra = he lies on his belly
seÂīhiti peÂīlunaÂīwik'aÂīhtca = tomorrow he's going to lie on his belly
hapeÂīl'Érani = I'm lying on my belly
'aÂīyÃĪhÃĪpaÂīn'ÉhÉ yaÂīkanik'aÂīhtca = I'm coming back in a little while
taÂīrihkuk 'uk'eÂīhpukani = I make the stick crooked
tiÂītihtÂīÃĪk taÂīkoru hoÂīn'uhki = The river ends in a bay
raÂīmaÂīku saÂīhku = a chew of tobacco
ruÂīsat'ÃĪ = big briars, bear(?) briar, hedge used as fence (Fr. rosier)
yuÂīru hÃĪÂīhakani = I take a big breath
ruÂīinapaÂīnu leÂīhukani = it's very hot, I'm gasping
taÂīyimohku poÂīht'Éhkini = I cut the grass
teÂīlia hiÂīn'ahkini = I draw a picture
teÂīlia hiÂīnanik'aÂīhtca = I'm going to draw a picture
teÂīlia haÂīlapoÂīkani = I used to draw a picture
hiÂīp'iwanak'aÂīhtca = I'm going to want to dance
hiÂīp'eÂīrusa = I know how to dance
seÂīhitic hiÂīpu 'eÂīrusa yaÂīnik'aÂīhtca = tomorrow I'm going to know how to dance
naÂīmupaÂīn'ÉhÉ haÂīrakani = a little bit I sing
yaÂītohk hÃĪÂīmawik'aÂīhtca = he's going to tame the little deer
taÂīyatohok hÃĪÂīm'uhki = the deer used to be tame
haÂīmit'ÃĪ and haÂīmito unknown to Sam
saÂītcik'uÂīsa = rain's coming, it's coming raining
taÂīminuk 'uhiÂīlahtanik'aÂīhtca = I'm going to squeeze the cat
ta'eÂīlutÉÂīrahtak 'uhiÂīlahtanik'aÂīhtca = I'm going to squeeze the orange
niÂīmupoÂīku = he used to clench it
ma haÂīp'ik'ahtca = shut up!, you're going to shut up
'ÃĪÂīckatÃĪracki n wÃĪÂīha a crack is open, spread apart adj kaÂīl'ÃĪhkini

tr.td: a crack is open, spread apart

div.p: 'iÂīmÄ 'uwÃĪÂīhan'aÂīhkini = I make a crack by opening something a little
seÂīhitic 'iÂīrici hÃĪÂīyakatik'aÂīhtca = tomorrow my nose is going to bleed
taÂīhkatomu taÂīwici 'aheÂīkunĘaÂīhkini = I mix flour + water
taÂīminuk 'uheÂīsun'aÂīrani = I'm dragging along the cat
taÂīhtc'ahtc heÂīmuhk'aÂīra = the new moon; the moon is renewing
hiÂīnukani = I'm moving along on the ground
taÂīrihkukÉra hiÂīn'ata = the wagon's moving on the ground
taÂīsak hiÂīnuhk'uÂīra = the dog's moving along on the ground
taÂīhkÉra haÂīiht hiÂīnukani = I'm moving along on the table
hiÂīna'eÂīrusa = I know how to read, write
riÂīhku-hiÂīrani = any kind of tree used for medicine
One kind is the rihkurÉÂīwalaÂīmihta, the bark of which sried makes a tea useful in curing fever; linden, Fr.
The root is boiled to make a tea with
Mash the leaves to make a plaster
waÂīitohk hÃĪÂīr'ik = look out! watch out!
toÂīnik tiÂīric piÂītahk' uÂīna uÂīwa = that man is going ahead
taÂīrku 'ukÉÂīsÄ tcoÂīluku = the tree when peeled leaks, bleeds, gives forth sap
hÉÂīsÄ huÂīma = vine with berries on
yiÂīcihÉÂīsÄ = vine sp. of which Sam has heard but doesn't know what it is.
hoÂīhtciku = he limps, he walks lame
hoÂīhtcipiÂītawik'aÂīhtca = he's going to walk lame
hoÂīlun'aÂīhkini = I been swap, I swapped
huÂīhpi = some kind of winter bird, petit gris
taÂīpahitaku taÂīri haÂīihta paÂīt'uhki = the lightening struck the house
taÂīpahita haÂīhtcutiÂīpusa taÂīhaL kitc 'aÂīra = sulphur lies in the ground
cuÂīririÂīna = root used for making red powder
taÂīyimohkuk 'usaÂīk'iki wiyaÂīhtutik'aÂīhtca = If you eat that weed it's going to poison you
yaÂīmahk'ura = he's putting on fine clothes, dressing up grand
yaÂīma'iÂīrawik'ahÄa = he's going to wear fine clothes
yaÂīmatik'aÂīhÄa = she's going to dress up fine
tiÂīraka ik'aÂīra = I have a ribbon
tiÂīraka yuÂīru laÂīp'ÉÂīhkini = I bought a long ribbon
tiÂīraka n saÂīhku cardnum laÂīp'ÉÂīhkini v.I
'uÂīwi laÂīspÄŊ 'ukaÂīhahtanik'aÂīhÄa = I'm going to pay him money
kaÂīhpuhk'uta = he (pinches) grabs hold of as scissors on cloth
kaÂīÅĄua meÂīli = black moss / grows on a tree
kÃĪÂīralaÂīhpa = spotted (of spots on a kettle)
tÃĪÂīrackÉÂītÄ toÂīsu kÃĪÂīrataÂīp'ahkini = I scattered + planted cabbage seed
maÂīisahk naÂī'aÂīrani = I lie on one side
yaÂīruhk kaÂīr'iwaÂīna = I want to sharpen an ax by grinding
Take the bark and mash it to a powder and feed a sick horse with it.
(S connects last two syll with " water ")
wicÄŊ koÂīm'ÉÂīhkini = I take a sip of water
'iÂīmÄ koÂītcunik'aÂīhÄa = I'm going to shorten something
> The Old Woman Who Never Died... 51-55
{ The Mounds Near Marksville, La... 61 (of III 122)
>{ The Origin of Corn... 63-65 (see II 159-161) (told a 2nd time)
